Case title: Deep Research agents for policy-literature reconnaissance
Short description: Adapt long-horizon web-research agents to frozen or live policy-literature tasks and examine when human source-boundary decisions still dominate.
Empirical unit of analysis: Long-horizon research traces, retrieved sources, and final policy-literature memos
Candidate roles tested: Corpus pluralist; protocol and provenance architect
Key typology question: Can agentic web research reduce human literature work without silently narrowing the evidentiary universe?
Observable artifacts / traces: Browser traces, source lists, hallucination markers, final memos
Key outcomes observed: Source diversity, claim grounding, retrieval depth, forgetting or drift
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Agentic research produces equally diverse and well-grounded corpora without human boundary setting
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Current deep-research benchmarks are not social-science-specific and often use frozen web corpora
Why this case has high leverage: Useful to stress-test corpus pluralism under live or semi-live web search conditions.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
